new_classes.label_en: Gin in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22085019)
new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22085019)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin, a spirit produced from rectified grain spirit or ethyl alcohol by distillation in the presence of juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise), presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 50 19 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 22085019 are: (1) it is Gin (a spirit produced from rectified grain spirit or ethyl alcohol by distillation with juniper berries and other aromatics) — captured by subclassing eucn:Gin and the hasAromatisationProcess restriction on JuniperBerryAromatisation; (2) it is presented in containers of MORE than 2 litres — captured by the decimalRange restriction on maxContainerVolumeL with minExclusive 2; (3) the CN code string is asserted; (4) disjointness from GinSmallContainer (≤ 2L) is expressed via complement restriction. A small gap remains: the legal text mentions additional aromatics (coriander, angelica root, anise) beyond juniper berries, which is only partially represented through the JuniperBerryAromatisation class (which covers juniper but not the other named botanicals explicitly). The production base (rectified grain spirit / ethyl alcohol) is also only indirectly captured via the parent Gin/Spirit classes, as no dedicated GrainDistillation restriction is placed on GinLargeContainer specifically. These minor gaps reduce coverage slightly from 1.0.
